about.body:
A Java Edition world-layer structure schematic generator and viewer made by stanlapru.

It reads level.dat-adjacent Anvil region files, displays one coordinate layer at a time, supports block exclusions, zooming, panning, and PNG export.

This program is not shipped with Minecraft textures. You must select a texture atlas JSON found at
'assets/minecraft/atlases/blocks.json'
 or the texture folder found at
'assets/minecraft/textures/block'
from an extracted Minecraft .jar file of the desired game version. You can extract this .jar file by making a copy of that file, renaming the extension to .zip, and unzipping it.

You may also opt to select your own texture atlas and textures from a resource pack using the corresponding buttons.

Version 1.0.0. Created by stanlap (c) 2026

bounds.title: Boundaries (inclusive)
dialog.first_launch.body:
MAKE SURE TO READ THIS:

1. In order to use this tool, you must first open a Java Edition world by selecting its level.dat.
2. Then, enter inclusive structure bounds and optionally name the structure. Note that establishing proper boundaries can take a few attempts on your part.
3. This program is not shipped with Minecraft textures. You must select a texture atlas JSON found at
'assets/minecraft/atlases/blocks.json'
 or the texture folder found at
'assets/minecraft/textures/block'
from an extracted Minecraft .jar file of the desired game version. You can extract this .jar file by making a copy of that file, renaming the extension to .zip, and unzipping it.
You may also opt to select your own texture atlas and textures from a resource pack using the corresponding buttons.
4. Check the Options menu to customize the program appearance and features to your liking.
